name: Enode EV & Energy Device API
what_it_does: Unified API for connecting and controlling EV vehicles, home chargers, heat pumps, smart batteries, and solar inverters — enabling energy management and smart charging across 100+ vehicle brands and energy devices.
use_cases: Reading real-time EV state-of-charge and range data across 100+ vehicle brands
Scheduling smart charging sessions to optimize for cheap or green electricity
Aggregating residential energy flexibility for demand response programs
Building EV fleet management systems with multi-brand vehicle data
Connecting home energy storage and solar systems for grid optimization
not_for: Non-EV vehicle telematics — Enode focuses on electric vehicles and energy devices
Commercial charging network management (use ChargePoint API instead)
Real-time telematics at sub-second polling rates
best_when: An agent needs to read and control EV vehicles or home energy devices across multiple brands from a single normalized API without per-brand integrations.
avoid_when: You need combustion vehicle telematics or access to charging network infrastructure APIs.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for vehicle owner authorization (user must link their vehicle account). API key for Enode dashboard authentication. Scopes for read, charge, and control actions. Webhook secrets for payload verification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free sandbox with test vehicles; production requires paid plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based per linked vehicle; contact sales for production pricing
pricing.notes: Startup-friendly pricing with sandbox access. Production pricing based on linked vehicle/device count. Focused on EV fleet and energy management use cases.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by endpoint; vehicle data cached at source polling intervals

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API well-documented with OpenAPI-style references. Webhook delivery with retry logic. Vehicle state updates are event-driven via webhooks — polling is supported but not recommended for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Vehicle data reads are idempotent. Charge start/stop commands are not — duplicate commands may create conflicts depending on vehicle state.
known_agent_gotchas: Vehicle data freshness depends on OEM polling intervals (30 seconds to 15 minutes)
Charge command success depends on vehicle connectivity and OEM cloud availability
OAuth authorization required per vehicle owner — cannot automate without user interaction
Brand coverage varies — verify specific vehicle brand support before building workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 for vehicle owner authorization. GDPR compliant. Webhook signature verification. TLS enforced. EU-based company with strong data protection focus. SOC2 compliance.
